title: Apply for PhD Scholarship
steps: Visit the PhD section on the main campus (Building A, Floor 2)
Check the scholarship noticeboard for available opportunities
Collect the scholarship application form from the secretary
Prepare required documents: national ID, academic transcripts, recommendation letters
Fill in the application form completely and accurately
Submit the completed form with all documents before the deadline
Await notification via your university email within 3 weeks

title: Course Registration
steps: Log in to the student portal at portal.university.edu
Navigate to 'Academic Services' → 'Course Registration'
Select the current semester from the dropdown
Browse available courses and check for pre-requisites
Add desired courses to your basket (max 21 credit hours)
Confirm your selection and submit registration
Print or save your registration confirmation as proof

title: Defer a Semester
steps: Obtain the deferral request form from the student affairs office
Fill in the form with your student ID, reason for deferral, and intended return date
Attach supporting documents (medical certificate, work contract, etc.) if applicable
Get your academic advisor's signature on the form
Submit the form to the student affairs office at least 2 weeks before semester start
Pay any applicable administration fees at the finance office
Receive a deferral confirmation letter — keep this for your records

title: Request an Official Transcript
steps: Go to the Registrar's Office (Admin Building, Ground Floor)
Fill in the transcript request form
Specify the number of copies required and the destination (e.g., employer, embassy)
Pay the transcript fee at the cashier window (receipt required)
Submit the form along with your payment receipt
Transcripts are ready for collection within 5 working days
Collect your sealed transcript(s) in person with your student ID

title: Appeal an Exam Grade
steps: Collect the grade appeal form from the examination office
Submit the form within 7 days of results publication
Pay the non-refundable appeal fee (refunded if appeal is successful)
Your paper will be re-marked by a different examiner
Appeal results are communicated within 2 weeks via email
If unsuccessful, you may escalate to the academic appeals board

title: Get a Library Card
steps: Visit the main university library during opening hours (8am–8pm weekdays)
Bring your student ID and one passport-size photo
Fill in the library membership form at the help desk
Library card is issued on the spot at no charge
Card gives access to all campus libraries, digital resources, and inter-library loans

title: Apply for Graduation
steps: Confirm you have completed all required credit hours with your academic advisor
Settle any outstanding fees or library fines
Submit the graduation application form via the student portal
The registrar will audit your academic record within 2 weeks
If approved, you will receive a clearance confirmation email
Collect your graduation gown and invitation from the student affairs office
Attend the graduation ceremony on the scheduled date

title: Replace Lost Student ID
steps: Report the lost ID at the campus security office and get a reference number
Fill in the ID replacement form at the student affairs office
Pay the replacement fee at the finance office
Submit the form with your payment receipt and a passport photo
New ID is ready within 3 working days
Collect your new ID card from student affairs
